title: Home — Mobile Amateur Radio
description: The encyclopedic reference for mobile HF and VHF installation, antennas, bonding, wiring, RFI, and more.

title: ABCs of Mobile HF
description: A no-nonsense guide to getting started with mobile HF amateur radio. Planning, equipment selection, installation fundamentals, and the mistakes to avoid.
headings: Wiring: The Foundation You Cannot Skip
What Has Changed Since This Was Written

title: Alternators Batteries
description: Complete guide to alternators, batteries, auxiliary power, and electrical capacity for mobile amateur radio installations. Covers alternator ratings, auxiliary batteries, isolators, and battery monitoring systems.
headings: Modern Considerations: EVs, Hybrids, and 48-Volt Systems

title: Amplifiers
description: Should you add an HF amplifier to your mobile installation? Honest guidance on when amplifiers help, what to avoid, and why your antenna system matters more than raw power.
headings: Power Wiring: The Numbers You Need
Drive Level: This Is Where Finals Go to Die
Common Mode: The Really Big Problem
Antennas to Avoid with an Amplifier
A Note on Permanent SWR Meters

title: Antenna Cap Hat
description: The definitive guide to capacity hats (cap hats) for HF mobile antennas: history, design principles, correct mounting position, the three-loop design, and efficiency improvements.

title: Commercial Antennas
description: Evaluating commercial HF mobile antennas: screwdrivers, short stubby antennas, spirally wound designs, monoband antennas, and what to look for and avoid when buying.
headings: Ford F-Series and Other Aluminum-Body Vehicles

title: Antenna Controllers
description: Complete guide to mobile HF antenna controllers: turn-counting vs. SWR-sensing types, proper choke placement, and why most factory chokes are inadequate.

title: Antenna Efficiency
description: Understanding HF mobile antenna efficiency: ground losses, radiation resistance, coil Q, calculating efficiency, and bandwidth. Why most mobile antennas are far less efficient than operators assume.

title: Antenna Matching
description: How to properly match a mobile HF antenna to 50 ohms using shunt coils, UNUNs, and stub matching. Why chasing low SWR instead of zero reactance is a mistake.
headings: Reactance vs. SWR: The Critical Distinction
160 Meter Coverage: When the Shunt Coil Is Not Enough

title: Antenna Mounts
description: Complete guide to HF mobile antenna mounting options: mag mounts, ballmounts, bed and clip mounts, foldover mounts, post mounts, trailer hitch mounts, and more.
headings: Bed, Clamp, Clip, Lip, Seam, and Trunk Mounts

title: Antenna Myths
description: Busting the most persistent myths about HF mobile antennas: SWR, gain, bandwidth, coil Q, ground loss, DX claims, NVIS, coaxial myths, and more.

title: Antenna Problems
description: Diagnosing and solving common mobile HF antenna problems — ground straps, motor lead choking, coax issues, screwdriver antenna maintenance, and RFI troubleshooting.
headings: Motor Lead Choking — The Most Critical and Most Neglected Step
DC Motor Polarity — Trust but Verify
Coax Selection — Bigger Is Not Better

title: Antenna Shootouts
description: What antenna shootouts actually measure, the variables that invalidate most results, winning and losing strategies, and a better measurement approach using SNR rather than field strength.

title: Audio Filtering Speakers
description: Audio filtering, DSP speakers, powered speakers, and plain speakers for mobile amateur radio. Speaker placement for hearing safety and noise reduction.

title: Auto-Couplers
description: Auto-couplers in mobile HF installations: what they can and cannot do, RF voltage hazards, mounting requirements, efficiency limitations, and how to improve auto-coupler/whip performance.

title: Bonding
description: Complete guide to RF bonding your vehicle for mobile amateur radio. Learn why bonding matters, what to bond, and how to do it right with flat copper braid.
headings: What Is Bonding, and Why Should You Care?
The Exhaust System: A Special Problem
Aluminum, Composites, and the Modern Vehicle

title: Cables Interfacing
description: Remote cables, microphone connections, modular plugs and jacks, and interfacing for mobile amateur radio transceivers. Why CAT5 is not a substitute and how to avoid expensive mistakes.
headings: If You Just Have to Make Your Own

title: Coax PL-259s
description: Comprehensive guide to coaxial cable selection, PL-259 connector assembly, crimp connectors, weather sealing, and common mode choke construction for mobile amateur radio.

title: Coil Adjustment
description: How to properly adjust screwdriver and resonator antennas for optimal mobile HF performance. Covers tuning procedures, whip length, weather effects, and common mistakes.
headings: Always Tune with the Engine Running
Resonance vs. Lowest SWR: They Are Not the Same
Setting Resonant Points Band by Band

title: Common Mode Currents
description: Understanding and eliminating common mode currents in mobile HF installations. Ferrite choke construction, placement, and measurement techniques for amateur radio operators.
headings: The Silent Killer of Mobile Performance
The Coax Choke: Building One That Works
The Receive Side of the Problem
Special Cases: 160 Meters and Problem Mounts

title: Controlling Static
description: Understanding and controlling the many forms of static in mobile amateur radio installations: atmospheric static, rain static, dust static, corona loss, and static drains.

title: Digital Electronics
description: Modern vehicle digital electronics and amateur radio: OBD-II, B-CAN data bus systems, EMC compliance, RFI ingress and egress, battery monitoring systems, and what not to do with ferrite chokes.
headings: Basics: Amateur Radio and Warranty Claims

title: Glossary
description: Comprehensive glossary of terms used in mobile amateur radio installation, including antenna, electrical, vehicle, and digital communications terminology.

title: Grounds, RF DC
description: Understanding RF and DC grounds in mobile amateur radio. Ground plane basics, mounting location effects on efficiency, and what a mobile ground plane actually is.

title: Home Brew Things
description: DIY projects for mobile amateur radio: relay boxes, loading coils, masts, brackets, amplifier bypass circuits, debouncer circuits, antenna switches, and more.

title: How to Wind a Choke
description: Step-by-step procedure for winding a motor lead and reed switch RF choke using Mix 31 ferrite split beads for mobile HF antenna controllers. Correct materials, wire, technique, and turn count.

title: Hybrid Electric Vehicles
description: Amateur radio installation in hybrid and electric vehicles: RF noise from inverter systems, high-voltage hazards, warranty implications, and what you can and cannot do.
headings: Modern Considerations: The Evolving EV Landscape

title: Installation Notes
description: Practical guidance on installing mobile HF radio equipment in vehicles — antenna mounting, transceiver placement, wiring, and the compromises involved.
headings: The Hard Truth About Mobile Installation
Yes, You Will Need to Drill Holes
Airbag Safety — This Is Not Optional Reading
Truck Bed Boxes as Antenna Mounts

title: Amateur Mobile Insurance
description: Amateur radio mobile insurance — what vehicle policies cover, notification requirements, permanent installation rules, distraction laws, and how to protect your equipment.

title: Miniature Radios
description: Comprehensive buyer's guide to miniature and mobile HF transceivers. Honest assessments of Icom, Yaesu, Kenwood, Alinco, and Elecraft mobile radios for HF operation.
headings: A Few Words About AM Operation

title: Neat Gadgets
description: Tools, accessories, and gadgets Alan Applegate K0BG recommends for mobile amateur radio: antenna analyzers, VNAs, RF meters, auto-off devices, and more.

title: OTR RV Installations
description: Practical guidance for installing HF amateur radio in over-the-road trucks and recreational vehicles, covering antenna mounting, grounding, RFI suppression, and wiring challenges unique to large vehicles.
headings: The Body Problem: It Isn't Steel
Power Systems: The Good News and the Bad
RFI Suppression: Split Beads Are Not Optional
Antenna Mounting: Where the Compromises Live

Stationary Operation: Forget the Tripod Fantasy

title: Portable Operation
description: Stationary mobile and portable amateur radio operation — power sources, battery management, antenna choices, pedestrian mobile, and special considerations.

title: RFI Problems
description: Identifying and solving RFI problems in mobile amateur radio installations. Covers ignition noise, data bus interference, alternator whine, and vehicle electronics.
headings: The Major Bane of Mobile Operation
Navigation Systems, LEDs, and Fluorescent Lighting

title: Safety
description: Comprehensive safety guide for mobile amateur radio installations. Covers airbag hazards, RF exposure, electrical safety, mounting safety, driving distraction, and vehicle-specific dangers.

title: Signal to Noise Ratio
description: Signal-to-noise ratio for mobile HF operation. S/N formula, AGC behavior, antenna Q, bonding improvements, and the ARRL IC-7000 measurement table.

title: Transmit Audio
description: SSB transmit audio for mobile amateur radio. Microphone technique, ALC operation, IMD, speech compression, dynamic range, and why less processing means better audio.

title: Tricks of the Trade
description: Practical tips, shortcuts, and hard-won wisdom for mobile amateur radio installations — bonding materials, fasteners, tools, coax, antenna care, and more.
headings: Car Polish and RF: A Hidden Problem

title: VHF/UHF Options
description: Mobile VHF and UHF installation guide covering NMO mounts, mag mounts, antenna selection, RFI challenges, and why the same installation principles that apply to HF matter just as much above 50 MHz.
headings: Wide SWR Bandwidth Means Lossy Antenna

title: What I Use
description: K0BG's personal mobile amateur radio installation — Scorpion 680 antenna, SGC SG500 amplifier, Icom IC-7000, cap hat, and controller boxes in a Honda Ridgeline.

title: Wiring
description: How to properly wire your mobile amateur radio installation. Dedicated power leads, correct wire gauge, fusing, connectors, and cable routing for HF mobile.
